ogTitle: North Beach vs North Boyanup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Beach and North Boyanup.
heading: North Beach vs North Boyanup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Beach, Western Australia and North Boyanup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Boyanup has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to North Beach (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Beach or North Boyanup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Beach has a flood risk score of 40/100, while North Boyanup scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Beach scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. North Boyanup scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Beach or North Boyanup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Boyanup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
